content: Personal-finance AI categorization UX norm is human-in-the-loop: the classifier pre-fills a suggested category in a review row, the user confirms or overrides inline, and only a confirmation persists/learns the pattern (never auto-commit). Best practice surfaces provenance (memory-matched vs AI-suggested) and a confidence hint, sorts low-confidence rows to the top for attention, and batches an entire statement's unseen descriptors into one model call rather than per-transaction live streaming. Over-engineering to avoid for a single-user app: fine-tuning, multi-model voting/ensembles, per-keystroke streaming, and auto-applying AI results without confirmation.
